It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. The tumulus is designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "'''Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum'''" (狹城盾列池後陵) and attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], the 13th emperor of Japan.
== Overview == Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Himiko's niece, {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}). The tumulus faces south and measures 218.5 meters in length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of 132 meters and a height of 19 meters, while the front trapezoidal mound measures 121 meters in width and 16 meters in height. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers, with extensive use of fukiishi, giving it the name "Ishizuka" (stone mound).
=== Moat === The moat surrounding the tumulus is relatively narrow, except for the front mound. To the east, the moat narrows significantly, with the rear mound's moat of the adjacent Saki Ryozan Kofun encroaching into the constricted area. This overlap suggests that Saki Ryozan Kofun predates Ishizuka-yama Kofun. The close proximity implies a possible familial or significant relationship between the buried individuals. Three satellite tombs (陪塚), designated as I, Ro, and Ho, are located along the northern to northeastern perimeter.
=== Burial Chamber === Historical records from the Edo period suggest that the rear mound contains a {{ill|tateana-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a {{ill|long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, jewels, and swords are believed to have been looted, although details remain unclear.
=== Interred_individual === While the tumulus is attributed to Emperor Seimu, it was historically misidentified as the burial site of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], according to the *Shoku Nihongi* (*Continued Chronicles of Japan*). In 843 CE, a notable supernatural event led to the reclassification of the tombs: the northern tumulus was identified as Empress Jingū's, and the southern one as Emperor Seimu's.
=== Historical Theft === The *Fusō Ryakki* documents a significant looting incident in 1063, when monks from [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] desecrated the tumulus, resulting in the exile of 16 individuals. In the late Edo period, further thefts occurred in 1844 and 1848, leading to the confiscation of numerous magatama (comma-shaped beads). The perpetrators were harshly punished, including public execution, reflecting the Tokugawa shogunate's emphasis on preserving imperial mausolea during the political consolidation of the Bakumatsu era.
=== Construction Period === Based on its proximity to other tumuli and its architectural features, Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is believed to date to the late 4th century.<ref name="#1"/><ref>{{Cite book|title=Invitation to Imperial Mausolea|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=37–47}}</ref>
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History 3|author=Shun'ō Koizumi|editor=Nara Prefecture Historical Committee|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|year=1995|pages=230}} * {{Cite book|title=Imperial Mausoleum Kofun Overview|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=117–124}} * {{Cite book|title=Bakumatsu Excavation Incidents|author=Masahiro Mogi|publisher=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|year=1997|pages=370–377}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/013/index.html Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] * {{Commonscat|Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun}}

The tumulus is constructed in three tiers, with extensive use of fukiishi, giving it the name "Ishizuka" (stone mound).

To the east, the moat narrows significantly, with the rear mound's moat of the adjacent Saki Ryozan Kofun encroaching into the constricted area. This overlap suggests that Saki Ryozan Kofun predates Ishizuka-yama Kofun. The close proximity implies a possible familial or significant relationship between the buried individuals. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun]][[ja:佐紀石塚山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|location={{ill|Misasagi-chō|WD=Q85879846}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.700136|N|135.787403|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672}}|part_of={{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11383491]] Wikidata name: Saki Ishizukayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun (top left), Saki Ryozan Kofun (top right), and Saki Takatsuka Kofun (bottom left) (1979) {{国土航空写真}}]] [[File:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Seimu Tennō's Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum}}]]
'''Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun''' (佐紀石塚山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Yamashiro Town|ja|山陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Town|qq=}}, Nara City, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. The tumulus is designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "'''Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum'''" (狹城盾列池後陵) and attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], the 13th emperor of Japan.
== Overview == Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Himiko's niece, {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}). The tumulus faces south and measures 218.5 meters in length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of 132 meters and a height of 19 meters, while the front trapezoidal mound measures 121 meters in width and 16 meters in height. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers, with extensive use of fukiishi, giving it the name "Ishizuka" (stone mound).
=== Moat === The moat surrounding the tumulus is relatively narrow, except for the front mound. To the east, the moat narrows significantly, with the rear mound's moat of the adjacent Saki Ryozan Kofun encroaching into the constricted area. This overlap suggests that Saki Ryozan Kofun predates Ishizuka-yama Kofun. The close proximity implies a possible familial or significant relationship between the buried individuals. Three satellite tombs (陪塚), designated as I, Ro, and Ho, are located along the northern to northeastern perimeter.
=== Burial Chamber === Historical records from the Edo period suggest that the rear mound contains a {{ill|tateana-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a {{ill|long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, jewels, and swords are believed to have been looted, although details remain unclear.
=== Interred_individual === While the tumulus is attributed to Emperor Seimu, it was historically misidentified as the burial site of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], according to the *Shoku Nihongi* (*Continued Chronicles of Japan*). In 843 CE, a notable supernatural event led to the reclassification of the tombs: the northern tumulus was identified as Empress Jingū's, and the southern one as Emperor Seimu's.
=== Historical Theft === The *Fusō Ryakki* documents a significant looting incident in 1063, when monks from [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] desecrated the tumulus, resulting in the exile of 16 individuals. In the late Edo period, further thefts occurred in 1844 and 1848, leading to the confiscation of numerous magatama (comma-shaped beads). The perpetrators were harshly punished, including public execution, reflecting the Tokugawa shogunate's emphasis on preserving imperial mausolea during the political consolidation of the Bakumatsu era.
=== Construction Period === Based on its proximity to other tumuli and its architectural features, Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is believed to date to the late 4th century.<ref name="#1"/><ref>{{Cite book|title=Invitation to Imperial Mausolea|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=37–47}}</ref>
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History 3|author=Shun'ō Koizumi|editor=Nara Prefecture Historical Committee|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|year=1995|pages=230}} * {{Cite book|title=Imperial Mausoleum Kofun Overview|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=117–124}} * {{Cite book|title=Bakumatsu Excavation Incidents|author=Masahiro Mogi|publisher=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|year=1997|pages=370–377}}

Three satellite tombs (陪塚), designated as I, Ro, and Ho, are located along the northern to northeastern perimeter.
=== Burial Chamber === Historical records from the Edo period suggest that the rear mound contains a {{ill|tateana-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a {{ill|long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, jewels, and swords are believed to have been looted, although details remain unclear.
=== Interred_individual === While the tumulus is attributed to Emperor Seimu, it was historically misidentified as the burial site of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], according to the *Shoku Nihongi* (*Continued Chronicles of Japan*). In 843 CE, a notable supernatural event led to the reclassification of the tombs: the northern tumulus was identified as Empress Jingū's, and the southern one as Emperor Seimu's.
=== Historical Theft === The *Fusō Ryakki* documents a significant looting incident in 1063, when monks from [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] desecrated the tumulus, resulting in the exile of 16 individuals. In the late Edo period, further thefts occurred in 1844 and 1848, leading to the confiscation of numerous magatama (comma-shaped beads). The perpetrators were harshly punished, including public execution, reflecting the Tokugawa shogunate's emphasis on preserving imperial mausolea during the political consolidation of the Bakumatsu era.
=== Construction Period === Based on its proximity to other tumuli and its architectural features, Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is believed to date to the late 4th century.<ref name="#1"/><ref>{{Cite book|title=Invitation to Imperial Mausolea|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=37–47}}</ref>
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History 3|author=Shun'ō Koizumi|editor=Nara Prefecture Historical Committee|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|year=1995|pages=230}} * {{Cite book|title=Imperial Mausoleum Kofun Overview|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=117–124}} * {{Cite book|title=Bakumatsu Excavation Incidents|author=Masahiro Mogi|publisher=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|year=1997|pages=370–377}}

'''Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun''' (佐紀石塚山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Yamashiro Town|ja|山陵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Town|qq=}}, Nara City, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. The tumulus is designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "'''Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum'''" (狹城盾列池後陵) and attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], the 13th emperor of Japan.
== Overview == Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Himiko's niece, {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}). The tumulus faces south and measures 218.5 meters in length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of 132 meters and a height of 19 meters, while the front trapezoidal mound measures 121 meters in width and 16 meters in height. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers, with extensive use of fukiishi, giving it the name "Ishizuka" (stone mound).
=== Moat === The moat surrounding the tumulus is relatively narrow, except for the front mound. To the east, the moat narrows significantly, with the rear mound's moat of the adjacent Saki Ryozan Kofun encroaching into the constricted area. This overlap suggests that Saki Ryozan Kofun predates Ishizuka-yama Kofun. The close proximity implies a possible familial or significant relationship between the buried individuals. Three satellite tombs (陪塚), designated as I, Ro, and Ho, are located along the northern to northeastern perimeter.
=== Burial Chamber === Historical records from the Edo period suggest that the rear mound contains a {{ill|tateana-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a {{ill|long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, jewels, and swords are believed to have been looted, although details remain unclear.
=== Interred_individual === While the tumulus is attributed to Emperor Seimu, it was historically misidentified as the burial site of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], according to the *Shoku Nihongi* (*Continued Chronicles of Japan*). In 843 CE, a notable supernatural event led to the reclassification of the tombs: the northern tumulus was identified as Empress Jingū's, and the southern one as Emperor Seimu's.
=== Historical Theft === The *Fusō Ryakki* documents a significant looting incident in 1063, when monks from [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] desecrated the tumulus, resulting in the exile of 16 individuals. In the late Edo period, further thefts occurred in 1844 and 1848, leading to the confiscation of numerous magatama (comma-shaped beads). The perpetrators were harshly punished, including public execution, reflecting the Tokugawa shogunate's emphasis on preserving imperial mausolea during the political consolidation of the Bakumatsu era.
=== Construction Period === Based on its proximity to other tumuli and its architectural features, Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is believed to date to the late 4th century.<ref name="#1"/><ref>{{Cite book|title=Invitation to Imperial Mausolea|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=37–47}}</ref>

'''Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun''' (佐紀石塚山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Yamashiro Town|ja|山陵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Town|qq=}}, Nara City, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. The tumulus is designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "'''Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum'''" (狹城盾列池後陵) and attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], the 13th emperor of Japan.
== Overview == Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Himiko's niece, {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}). The tumulus faces south and measures 218.5 meters in length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of 132 meters and a height of 19 meters, while the front trapezoidal mound measures 121 meters in width and 16 meters in height. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers, with extensive use of fukiishi, giving it the name "Ishizuka" (stone mound).
=== Moat === The moat surrounding the tumulus is relatively narrow, except for the front mound. To the east, the moat narrows significantly, with the rear mound's moat of the adjacent Saki Ryozan Kofun encroaching into the constricted area. This overlap suggests that Saki Ryozan Kofun predates Ishizuka-yama Kofun. The close proximity implies a possible familial or significant relationship between the buried individuals. Three satellite tombs (陪塚), designated as I, Ro, and Ho, are located along the northern to northeastern perimeter.
=== Burial Chamber === Historical records from the Edo period suggest that the rear mound contains a {{ill|tateana-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a {{ill|long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, jewels, and swords are believed to have been looted, although details remain unclear.
=== Interred_individual === While the tumulus is attributed to Emperor Seimu, it was historically misidentified as the burial site of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], according to the *Shoku Nihongi* (*Continued Chronicles of Japan*). In 843 CE, a notable supernatural event led to the reclassification of the tombs: the northern tumulus was identified as Empress Jingū's, and the southern one as Emperor Seimu's.
=== Historical Theft === The *Fusō Ryakki* documents a significant looting incident in 1063, when monks from [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] desecrated the tumulus, resulting in the exile of 16 individuals. In the late Edo period, further thefts occurred in 1844 and 1848, leading to the confiscation of numerous magatama (comma-shaped beads). The perpetrators were harshly punished, including public execution, reflecting the Tokugawa shogunate's emphasis on preserving imperial mausolea during the political consolidation of the Bakumatsu era.
=== Construction Period === Based on its proximity to other tumuli and its architectural features, Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is believed to date to the late 4th century.<ref name="#1"/><ref>{{Cite book|title=Invitation to Imperial Mausolea|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=37–47}}</ref>
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History 3|author=Shun'ō Koizumi|editor=Nara Prefecture Historical Committee|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|year=1995|pages=230}} * {{Cite book|title=Imperial Mausoleum Kofun Overview|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=117–124}} * {{Cite book|title=Bakumatsu Excavation Incidents|author=Masahiro Mogi|publisher=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|year=1997|pages=370–377}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/013/index.html Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] * {{Commonscat|Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun}}

This overlap suggests that Saki Ryozan Kofun predates Ishizuka-yama Kofun. The close proximity implies a possible familial or significant relationship between the buried individuals. Three satellite tombs (陪塚), designated as I, Ro, and Ho, are located along the northern to northeastern perimeter.
=== Burial Chamber === Historical records from the Edo period suggest that the rear mound contains a {{ill|tateana-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a {{ill|long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, jewels, and swords are believed to have been looted, although details remain unclear.
=== Interred_individual === While the tumulus is attributed to Emperor Seimu, it was historically misidentified as the burial site of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], according to the *Shoku Nihongi* (*Continued Chronicles of Japan*). In 843 CE, a notable supernatural event led to the reclassification of the tombs: the northern tumulus was identified as Empress Jingū's, and the southern one as Emperor Seimu's.
=== Historical Theft === The *Fusō Ryakki* documents a significant looting incident in 1063, when monks from [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] desecrated the tumulus, resulting in the exile of 16 individuals. In the late Edo period, further thefts occurred in 1844 and 1848, leading to the confiscation of numerous magatama (comma-shaped beads). The perpetrators were harshly punished, including public execution, reflecting the Tokugawa shogunate's emphasis on preserving imperial mausolea during the political consolidation of the Bakumatsu era.
=== Construction Period === Based on its proximity to other tumuli and its architectural features, Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is believed to date to the late 4th century.<ref name="#1"/><ref>{{Cite book|title=Invitation to Imperial Mausolea|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=37–47}}</ref>
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History 3|author=Shun'ō Koizumi|editor=Nara Prefecture Historical Committee|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|year=1995|pages=230}} * {{Cite book|title=Imperial Mausoleum Kofun Overview|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=117–124}} * {{Cite book|title=Bakumatsu Excavation Incidents|author=Masahiro Mogi|publisher=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|year=1997|pages=370–377}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun]][[ja:佐紀石塚山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|location={{ill|Misasagi-chō|WD=Q85879846}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.700136|N|135.787403|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672}}|part_of={{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11383491]] Wikidata name: Saki Ishizukayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun (top left), Saki Ryozan Kofun (top right), and Saki Takatsuka Kofun (bottom left) (1979) {{国土航空写真}}]] [[File:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Seimu Tennō's Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum}}]]
'''Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun''' (佐紀石塚山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Yamashiro Town|ja|山陵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Town|qq=}}, Nara City, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. The tumulus is designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "'''Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum'''" (狹城盾列池後陵) and attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], the 13th emperor of Japan.
== Overview == Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Himiko's niece, {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}). The tumulus faces south and measures 218.5 meters in length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of 132 meters and a height of 19 meters, while the front trapezoidal mound measures 121 meters in width and 16 meters in height. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers, with extensive use of fukiishi, giving it the name "Ishizuka" (stone mound).
=== Moat === The moat surrounding the tumulus is relatively narrow, except for the front mound. To the east, the moat narrows significantly, with the rear mound's moat of the adjacent Saki Ryozan Kofun encroaching into the constricted area. This overlap suggests that Saki Ryozan Kofun predates Ishizuka-yama Kofun. The close proximity implies a possible familial or significant relationship between the buried individuals. Three satellite tombs (陪塚), designated as I, Ro, and Ho, are located along the northern to northeastern perimeter.
=== Burial Chamber === Historical records from the Edo period suggest that the rear mound contains a {{ill|tateana-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a {{ill|long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, jewels, and swords are believed to have been looted, although details remain unclear.
=== Interred_individual === While the tumulus is attributed to Emperor Seimu, it was historically misidentified as the burial site of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], according to the *Shoku Nihongi* (*Continued Chronicles of Japan*). In 843 CE, a notable supernatural event led to the reclassification of the tombs: the northern tumulus was identified as Empress Jingū's, and the southern one as Emperor Seimu's.
=== Historical Theft === The *Fusō Ryakki* documents a significant looting incident in 1063, when monks from [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] desecrated the tumulus, resulting in the exile of 16 individuals. In the late Edo period, further thefts occurred in 1844 and 1848, leading to the confiscation of numerous magatama (comma-shaped beads). The perpetrators were harshly punished, including public execution, reflecting the Tokugawa shogunate's emphasis on preserving imperial mausolea during the political consolidation of the Bakumatsu era.
=== Construction Period === Based on its proximity to other tumuli and its architectural features, Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is believed to date to the late 4th century.<ref name="#1"/><ref>{{Cite book|title=Invitation to Imperial Mausolea|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=37–47}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun]][[ja:佐紀石塚山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun]][[ja:佐紀石塚山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun]][[ja:佐紀石塚山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|location={{ill|Misasagi-chō|WD=Q85879846}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.700136|N|135.787403|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672}}|part_of={{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11383491]] Wikidata name: Saki Ishizukayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun (top left), Saki Ryozan Kofun (top right), and Saki Takatsuka Kofun (bottom left) (1979) {{国土航空写真}}]] [[File:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Seimu Tennō's Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum}}]]
'''Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun''' (佐紀石塚山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Yamashiro Town|ja|山陵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Town|qq=}}, Nara City, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. The tumulus is designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "'''Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum'''" (狹城盾列池後陵) and attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], the 13th emperor of Japan.
== Overview == Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Himiko's niece, {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}). The tumulus faces south and measures 218.5 meters in length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of 132 meters and a height of 19 meters, while the front trapezoidal mound measures 121 meters in width and 16 meters in height. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers, with extensive use of fukiishi, giving it the name "Ishizuka" (stone mound).
=== Moat === The moat surrounding the tumulus is relatively narrow, except for the front mound. To the east, the moat narrows significantly, with the rear mound's moat of the adjacent Saki Ryozan Kofun encroaching into the constricted area. This overlap suggests that Saki Ryozan Kofun predates Ishizuka-yama Kofun. The close proximity implies a possible familial or significant relationship between the buried individuals. Three satellite tombs (陪塚), designated as I, Ro, and Ho, are located along the northern to northeastern perimeter.
=== Burial Chamber === Historical records from the Edo period suggest that the rear mound contains a {{ill|tateana-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a {{ill|long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, jewels, and swords are believed to have been looted, although details remain unclear.
=== Interred_individual === While the tumulus is attributed to Emperor Seimu, it was historically misidentified as the burial site of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], according to the *Shoku Nihongi* (*Continued Chronicles of Japan*). In 843 CE, a notable supernatural event led to the reclassification of the tombs: the northern tumulus was identified as Empress Jingū's, and the southern one as Emperor Seimu's.
=== Historical Theft === The *Fusō Ryakki* documents a significant looting incident in 1063, when monks from [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] desecrated the tumulus, resulting in the exile of 16 individuals. In the late Edo period, further thefts occurred in 1844 and 1848, leading to the confiscation of numerous magatama (comma-shaped beads). The perpetrators were harshly punished, including public execution, reflecting the Tokugawa shogunate's emphasis on preserving imperial mausolea during the political consolidation of the Bakumatsu era.
=== Construction Period === Based on its proximity to other tumuli and its architectural features, Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is believed to date to the late 4th century.<ref name="#1"/><ref>{{Cite book|title=Invitation to Imperial Mausolea|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=37–47}}</ref>
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History 3|author=Shun'ō Koizumi|editor=Nara Prefecture Historical Committee|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|year=1995|pages=230}} * {{Cite book|title=Imperial Mausoleum Kofun Overview|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=117–124}} * {{Cite book|title=Bakumatsu Excavation Incidents|author=Masahiro Mogi|publisher=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|year=1997|pages=370–377}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/013/index.html Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] * {{Commonscat|Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun}}

To the east, the moat narrows significantly, with the rear mound's moat of the adjacent Saki Ryozan Kofun encroaching into the constricted area. This overlap suggests that Saki Ryozan Kofun predates Ishizuka-yama Kofun. The close proximity implies a possible familial or significant relationship between the buried individuals. Three satellite tombs (陪塚), designated as I, Ro, and Ho, are located along the northern to northeastern perimeter.
=== Burial Chamber === Historical records from the Edo period suggest that the rear mound contains a {{ill|tateana-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a {{ill|long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, jewels, and swords are believed to have been looted, although details remain unclear.
=== Interred_individual === While the tumulus is attributed to Emperor Seimu, it was historically misidentified as the burial site of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], according to the *Shoku Nihongi* (*Continued Chronicles of Japan*). In 843 CE, a notable supernatural event led to the reclassification of the tombs: the northern tumulus was identified as Empress Jingū's, and the southern one as Emperor Seimu's.
=== Historical Theft === The *Fusō Ryakki* documents a significant looting incident in 1063, when monks from [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] desecrated the tumulus, resulting in the exile of 16 individuals. In the late Edo period, further thefts occurred in 1844 and 1848, leading to the confiscation of numerous magatama (comma-shaped beads). The perpetrators were harshly punished, including public execution, reflecting the Tokugawa shogunate's emphasis on preserving imperial mausolea during the political consolidation of the Bakumatsu era.
=== Construction Period === Based on its proximity to other tumuli and its architectural features, Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is believed to date to the late 4th century.<ref name="#1"/><ref>{{Cite book|title=Invitation to Imperial Mausolea|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=37–47}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun]][[ja:佐紀石塚山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun]][[ja:佐紀石塚山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun]][[ja:佐紀石塚山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|location={{ill|Misasagi-chō|WD=Q85879846}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.700136|N|135.787403|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672}}|part_of={{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11383491]] Wikidata name: Saki Ishizukayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun (top left), Saki Ryozan Kofun (top right), and Saki Takatsuka Kofun (bottom left) (1979) {{国土航空写真}}]] [[File:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Seimu Tennō's Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum}}]]
'''Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun''' (佐紀石塚山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Yamashiro Town|ja|山陵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Town|qq=}}, Nara City, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. The tumulus is designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "'''Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum'''" (狹城盾列池後陵) and attributed to [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], the 13th emperor of Japan.
== Overview == Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Himiko's niece, {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}). The tumulus faces south and measures 218.5 meters in length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of 132 meters and a height of 19 meters, while the front trapezoidal mound measures 121 meters in width and 16 meters in height. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers, with extensive use of fukiishi, giving it the name "Ishizuka" (stone mound).
=== Moat === The moat surrounding the tumulus is relatively narrow, except for the front mound. To the east, the moat narrows significantly, with the rear mound's moat of the adjacent Saki Ryozan Kofun encroaching into the constricted area. This overlap suggests that Saki Ryozan Kofun predates Ishizuka-yama Kofun. The close proximity implies a possible familial or significant relationship between the buried individuals. Three satellite tombs (陪塚), designated as I, Ro, and Ho, are located along the northern to northeastern perimeter.
=== Burial Chamber === Historical records from the Edo period suggest that the rear mound contains a {{ill|tateana-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a {{ill|long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, jewels, and swords are believed to have been looted, although details remain unclear.
=== Interred_individual === While the tumulus is attributed to Emperor Seimu, it was historically misidentified as the burial site of [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]], according to the *Shoku Nihongi* (*Continued Chronicles of Japan*). In 843 CE, a notable supernatural event led to the reclassification of the tombs: the northern tumulus was identified as Empress Jingū's, and the southern one as Emperor Seimu's.
=== Historical Theft === The *Fusō Ryakki* documents a significant looting incident in 1063, when monks from [[:en:Kōfuku-ji|Kōfuku-ji]] desecrated the tumulus, resulting in the exile of 16 individuals. In the late Edo period, further thefts occurred in 1844 and 1848, leading to the confiscation of numerous magatama (comma-shaped beads). The perpetrators were harshly punished, including public execution, reflecting the Tokugawa shogunate's emphasis on preserving imperial mausolea during the political consolidation of the Bakumatsu era.
=== Construction Period === Based on its proximity to other tumuli and its architectural features, Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is believed to date to the late 4th century.<ref name="#1"/><ref>{{Cite book|title=Invitation to Imperial Mausolea|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=37–47}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun]][[ja:佐紀石塚山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun]][[ja:佐紀石塚山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun]][[ja:佐紀石塚山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|location={{ill|Misasagi-chō|WD=Q85879846}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.700136|N|135.787403|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672}}|part_of={{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11383491]] Wikidata name: Saki Ishizukayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun (top left), Saki Ryozan Kofun (top right), and Saki Takatsuka Kofun (bottom left) (1979) {{国土航空写真}}]] [[File:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Seimu Tennō's Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum}}]]
'''Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun''' (佐紀石塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yamashiro Town|ja|山陵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Town|qq=}}, Nara City, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. The tumulus is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} as the "'''Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum'''" (狹城盾列池後陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}}, the 13th emperor of Japan.
== Overview == Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Himiko's niece, {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}). The tumulus faces south and measures 218.5 meters in length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of 132 meters and a height of 19 meters, while the front trapezoidal mound measures 121 meters in width and 16 meters in height. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers, with extensive use of fukiishi, giving it the name "Ishizuka" (stone mound).
=== Moat === The moat surrounding the tumulus is relatively narrow, except for the front mound. To the east, the moat narrows significantly, with the rear mound's moat of the adjacent Saki Ryozan Kofun encroaching into the constricted area. This overlap suggests that Saki Ryozan Kofun predates Ishizuka-yama Kofun. The close proximity implies a possible familial or significant relationship between the buried individuals. Three satellite tombs (陪塚), designated as I, Ro, and Ho, are located along the northern to northeastern perimeter.
=== Burial Chamber === Historical records from the Edo period suggest that the rear mound contains a {{ill|tateana-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a {{ill|long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, jewels, and swords are believed to have been looted, although details remain unclear.
=== Interred_individual === While the tumulus is attributed to Emperor Seimu, it was historically misidentified as the burial site of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}, according to the *Shoku Nihongi* (*Continued Chronicles of Japan*). In 843 CE, a notable supernatural event led to the reclassification of the tombs: the northern tumulus was identified as Empress Jingū's, and the southern one as Emperor Seimu's.
=== Historical Theft === The *Fusō Ryakki* documents a significant looting incident in 1063, when monks from {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|en|Kōfuku-ji|WD=Q1070863|lt=Kōfuku-ji}} desecrated the tumulus, resulting in the exile of 16 individuals. In the late Edo period, further thefts occurred in 1844 and 1848, leading to the confiscation of numerous magatama (comma-shaped beads). The perpetrators were harshly punished, including public execution, reflecting the Tokugawa shogunate's emphasis on preserving imperial mausolea during the political consolidation of the Bakumatsu era.
=== Construction Period === Based on its proximity to other tumuli and its architectural features, Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is believed to date to the late 4th century.<ref name="#1"/><ref>{{Cite book|title=Invitation to Imperial Mausolea|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=37–47}}</ref>
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History 3|author=Shun'ō Koizumi|editor=Nara Prefecture Historical Committee|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|year=1995|pages=230}} * {{Cite book|title=Imperial Mausoleum Kofun Overview|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=117–124}} * {{Cite book|title=Bakumatsu Excavation Incidents|author=Masahiro Mogi|publisher=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|year=1997|pages=370–377}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/013/index.html Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum - Imperial Household Agency] * {{Commonscat|Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun}}
{{translated page|ja|佐紀石塚山古墳|version=94782860|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The tumulus faces south and measures 218.5 meters in length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of 132 meters and a height of 19 meters, while the front trapezoidal mound measures 121 meters in width and 16 meters in height. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers, with extensive use of fukiishi, giving it the name "Ishizuka" (stone mound).
=== Moat === The moat surrounding the tumulus is relatively narrow, except for the front mound. To the east, the moat narrows significantly, with the rear mound's moat of the adjacent Saki Ryozan Kofun encroaching into the constricted area. This overlap suggests that Saki Ryozan Kofun predates Ishizuka-yama Kofun. The close proximity implies a possible familial or significant relationship between the buried individuals. Three satellite tombs (陪塚), designated as I, Ro, and Ho, are located along the northern to northeastern perimeter.
=== Burial Chamber === Historical records from the Edo period suggest that the rear mound contains a {{ill|tateana-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a {{ill|long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, jewels, and swords are believed to have been looted, although details remain unclear.
=== Interred_individual === While the tumulus is attributed to Emperor Seimu, it was historically misidentified as the burial site of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, according to the *Shoku Nihongi* (*Continued Chronicles of Japan*). In 843 CE, a notable supernatural event led to the reclassification of the tombs: the northern tumulus was identified as Empress Jingū's, and the southern one as Emperor Seimu's.
=== Historical Theft === The *Fusō Ryakki* documents a significant looting incident in 1063, when monks from {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|en|Kōfuku-ji|WD=Q1070863|lt=Kōfuku-ji|1=Kōfuku-ji}} desecrated the tumulus, resulting in the exile of 16 individuals. In the late Edo period, further thefts occurred in 1844 and 1848, leading to the confiscation of numerous magatama (comma-shaped beads). The perpetrators were harshly punished, including public execution, reflecting the Tokugawa shogunate's emphasis on preserving imperial mausolea during the political consolidation of the Bakumatsu era.
=== Construction Period === Based on its proximity to other tumuli and its architectural features, Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is believed to date to the late 4th century.<ref name="#1"/><ref>{{Cite book|title=Invitation to Imperial Mausolea|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=37–47}}</ref>

The perpetrators were harshly punished, including public execution, reflecting the Tokugawa shogunate's emphasis on preserving imperial mausolea during the political consolidation of the Bakumatsu era.
=== Construction Period === Based on its proximity to other tumuli and its architectural features, Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is believed to date to the late 4th century.<ref name="#1"/><ref>{{Cite book|title=Invitation to Imperial Mausolea|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=37–47}}</ref>
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History 3|author=Shun'ō Koizumi|editor=Nara Prefecture Historical Committee|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|year=1995|pages=230}} * {{Cite book|title=Imperial Mausoleum Kofun Overview|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=117–124}} * {{Cite book|title=Bakumatsu Excavation Incidents|author=Masahiro Mogi|publisher=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|year=1997|pages=370–377}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun]][[ja:佐紀石塚山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun]][[ja:佐紀石塚山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:佐紀石塚山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|location={{ill|Misasagi-chō|WD=Q85879846|1=Misasagi-chō}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.700136|N|135.787403|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}| Interred_individual = {{ill|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}|part_of={{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}}}} [[File:790911 Ishiduka Misasagiyama Takatsuka.jpg|thumb|250px|Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun (top left), Saki Ryozan Kofun (top right), and Saki Takatsuka Kofun (bottom left) (1979) {{国土航空写真}}]] [[File:Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Seimu Tennō's Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum}}]]
'''Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun''' (佐紀石塚山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Yamashiro Town|ja|山陵町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Town|qq=}}, Nara City, Nara Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. The tumulus is designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as the "'''Saki-no-Tatanami-no-Ikejiri Mausoleum'''" (狹城盾列池後陵) and attributed to {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}, the 13th emperor of Japan.
== Overview == Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is situated adjacent to the {{ill|Saki Ryozan Kofun|ja|佐紀陵山古墳|1=Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Ryozan Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Himiko's niece, {{ill|Hibasu-hime|ja|日葉酢媛命|1=Hibasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime|qq=}}). The tumulus faces south and measures 218.5 meters in length. The rear circular mound has a diameter of 132 meters and a height of 19 meters, while the front trapezoidal mound measures 121 meters in width and 16 meters in height. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers, with extensive use of fukiishi, giving it the name "Ishizuka" (stone mound).
=== Moat === The moat surrounding the tumulus is relatively narrow, except for the front mound. To the east, the moat narrows significantly, with the rear mound's moat of the adjacent Saki Ryozan Kofun encroaching into the constricted area. This overlap suggests that Saki Ryozan Kofun predates Ishizuka-yama Kofun. The close proximity implies a possible familial or significant relationship between the buried individuals. Three satellite tombs (陪塚), designated as I, Ro, and Ho, are located along the northern to northeastern perimeter.
=== Burial Chamber === Historical records from the Edo period suggest that the rear mound contains a {{ill|tateana-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tateana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} housing a {{ill|long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/long coffin-shaped stone sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Artifacts such as mirrors, jewels, and swords are believed to have been looted, although details remain unclear.
=== Interred_individual === While the tumulus is attributed to Emperor Seimu, it was historically misidentified as the burial site of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, according to the *Shoku Nihongi* (*Continued Chronicles of Japan*). In 843 CE, a notable supernatural event led to the reclassification of the tombs: the northern tumulus was identified as Empress Jingū's, and the southern one as Emperor Seimu's.
=== Historical Theft === The *Fusō Ryakki* documents a significant looting incident in 1063, when monks from {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|en|Kōfuku-ji|WD=Q1070863|lt=Kōfuku-ji|1=Kōfuku-ji}} desecrated the tumulus, resulting in the exile of 16 individuals. In the late Edo period, further thefts occurred in 1844 and 1848, leading to the confiscation of numerous magatama (comma-shaped beads). The perpetrators were harshly punished, including public execution, reflecting the Tokugawa shogunate's emphasis on preserving imperial mausolea during the political consolidation of the Bakumatsu era.
=== Construction Period === Based on its proximity to other tumuli and its architectural features, Saki Ishizuka-yama Kofun is believed to date to the late 4th century.<ref name="#1"/><ref>{{Cite book|title=Invitation to Imperial Mausolea|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=37–47}}</ref>
== References == * {{Cite book|title=Nara Prefecture Archaeological History 3|author=Shun'ō Koizumi|editor=Nara Prefecture Historical Committee|publisher=Meicho Shuppan|year=1995|pages=230}} * {{Cite book|title=Imperial Mausoleum Kofun Overview|author=Hiroshi Mori|publisher=Chikuma Shobō|year=2011|pages=117–124}} * {{Cite book|title=Bakumatsu Excavation Incidents|author=Masahiro Mogi|publisher=Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|year=1997|pages=370–377}}
